data.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S4b e308: Claim-gate <-> plan-review ref-resolution coherence, shared boundary + ADR 0002). EMPTY findings list — recorded for traceability.]

4-bucket audit of this child's contribution; every absence cross-checked against the WHOLE eight-child roster (S1 6d56, S2 70c7, S2b b73c, S3 fa49, S4 30b3, S4b e308 THIS, S5 b7fa, S6 1ba5). The union of children covers every parent acceptance/success criterion; no uncovered parent criterion is attributable to this pairing.

Coverage map:
- AC1 (no gate reads working tree in attested mode; all read a snapshot at pinned SHA) — FULLY by S1+S3; this child REINFORCES the residual attestation-hashing path (its AC1: plan-review file_impact hashing + claim-gate re-check resolve through ONE shared boundary at the same pinned-SHA basis). Not an absence.
- AC2 (signed verdict binds verified_at_sha via manifest channel, reproducible, no prior-sig breakage) — FULLY by S4; this child REINFORCES reproducibility by guaranteeing the signed dependency map and the later freshness re-check hash at the SAME basis (negative test guards whole-HEAD divergence). Not an absence.
- AC3 (local mode reads in-place, cannot sign, documented back-out) — SPLIT S3+S4+S6. STRUCTURAL here; claims none. Not an absence.
- AC4 (concurrent same/different SHAs, cache+janitor reclaim under watermark without disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY S1+S2+S2b. STRUCTURAL. Not an absence.
- AC5 (private-repo fetch failure -> descriptive credential error) — FULLY S1+S6. STRUCTURAL. Not an absence.
- AC6 (portable macOS+Linux; no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — FULLY S1+S6. STRUCTURAL. Not an absence.
- AC7 (operational tunables configurable w/ documented defaults, S2/S3) — FULLY S2/S2b+S3+S6. STRUCTURAL. Not an absence.
- AC8 (ADR captures architecture + rejected alternatives: PID-lease, payload-schema SHA binding) — co-owned S2 (architecture) + S4 (manifest-vs-payload binding). THIS child carries an ADR 0002 update (shared-resolution consolidation + manifest cross-ref) which is adjacent doc, not the parent-AC8 architecture-ADR obligation; AC8 is covered by S2/S4. Not an absence.
- AC9 (all EIGHT enumerated child stories closed; make lint + tests green) — meta/STRUCTURAL; this child is itself one of the eight enumerated stories. Not an absence.

Sibling-delegation cross-checks: attested-signing manifest pin explicitly delegated OUT to S4 (present, owns AC1/AC2); snapshot/cache delegated to S1/S2/S2b (present). The negative test (this child's AC2) is in-scope and not delegated. No orphaned obligation.

Note (NOT a G3 finding): whether this child's file_impact (src/rebar/signing.py + docs/adr/0002) actually reaches the hashing sites it claims in AC1 (plan_review/attest.py) is a CONSISTENCY (G4) concern about which module the shared boundary lands in — not a G3 union-coverage absence, since the ref-coherence obligation IS claimed by a child (this one's AC1).

Conclusion: child cleanly covers the claim-gate<->plan-review ref-resolution coherence slice; reinforces parent AC1/AC2 and is the 8th enumerated story for AC9. No parent criterion left uncovered. No G3 finding for this pairing.
